projects.name: Secure Ops Manager (MCP)
projects.tagline: Go-based MCP server for auditable remote execution
projects.problem: Ops teams need to execute remote commands via LLMs securely, but exposing SSH credentials to AI models is a major security risk.
projects.solution: An MCP (Model Context Protocol) server built in Go that delegates SSH commands securely via local profiles and ssh-agent, keeping keys off the network.
projects.result: Reduces manual remote-operation errors and improves traceability for production maintenance tasks executed by AI.
projects.context: Built as a secure automation layer for day-to-day infrastructure operations with LLM-assisted workflows.
projects.role: I designed and implemented the entire tool from scratch in Go, focusing on the security model, profile resolution, and local append-only audit logs to trace LLM actions.
projects.architecture: MCP server in Go delegates to system ssh/ssh-add, resolves secure profiles, and writes local append-only audit logs.
decisions: Keep credentials out of repo and in local secure config
Require explicit confirm=YES before sudo execution
Fail fast for non-interactive auth instead of hanging
projects.impact: Safer remote automation, clearer operational accountability, and lower incident risk during production actions.
projects.next: Add signed audit exports and policy-driven command allowlists per profile.

projects.name: AutoDeploy Webhooks
projects.tagline: Lightweight PHP CI/CD webhook deployment service
projects.problem: Small teams lose development velocity due to manual, error-prone deployment steps across multiple servers.
projects.solution: A robust PHP-based webhook receiver that listens to GitHub pushes, validates payload signatures, and executes repo-scoped deployment shell chains securely.
projects.result: Cuts deployment friction and standardizes release operations with reproducible server updates.
projects.context: Productized internal deployment practice into a reusable service to reduce repetitive operational work.
projects.role: I was the sole architect and developer. I wrote the backend validation logic, configured Nginx, and built the self-updating mechanism to manage rollouts across distributed nodes.
projects.architecture: Webhook endpoint validates source and repo/key params, resolves deployment commands, executes hooks and logs outcomes.
decisions: Preserve safe defaults for pull/fetch/reset path
Ship self-update mechanism for distributed instances
projects.impact: Faster release cycles, fewer manual mistakes, and better consistency across independent deployments.
projects.next: Add immutable release channels and signed hook payload validation dashboard.

projects.name: Android Tunnel Hub
projects.tagline: Persistent reverse SSH tunneling Android app
projects.problem: Accessing unattended Android devices remotely (e.g. for IoT displays) is unreliable behind NATs and fragile with external tools.
projects.solution: A native Android application written in Kotlin that embeds a MINA SSHD server and maintains a highly resilient reverse SSH tunnel to a public server.
projects.result: Improves remote device operability and uptime with secure, app-native connectivity flows.
projects.context: Built to provide robust remote access for unattended devices without relying on ADB or Termux workflows.
projects.role: I built the entire Android app from the ground up, implementing foreground services for persistence, internal SSH key generation, and the Jetpack Compose UI.
projects.architecture: Foreground service supervises local SSH daemon and reverse tunnel lifecycle, with in-app key generation and connection state handling.
decisions: Bundle binaries and avoid external runtime dependencies
Use foreground service for resilience against process kills
Implement key-first auth and IPv4 fallback control
projects.impact: Higher reliability for remote maintenance scenarios and reduced setup complexity for non-expert operators.
projects.next: Add fleet device telemetry and policy-based tunnel profiles.

projects.name: Easy Casual
projects.problem: The business needed a modern product platform with faster delivery cycles and a clear path to production hardening.
projects.solution: A full-stack containerized platform using Next.js (frontend), Node.js (backend), and MongoDB, featuring a custom OTP (One-Time Password) authentication system.
projects.result: Accelerated MVP evolution while keeping a clean path for auth, scalability and production operations.
projects.context: Private client product with ongoing delivery and controlled access to source code.
projects.role: I led the technical architecture, implemented the full-stack codebase, configured the Docker + Nginx deployment strategy, and built the custom passwordless authentication flow.
projects.architecture: Frontend and backend containers behind Nginx reverse proxy, with OTP email auth and separate runtime configuration layers.
decisions: Keep app Docker-first for reproducible onboarding
Use OTP flow to simplify credential handling
Consolidate route and env conventions for maintainability
projects.impact: Reduced onboarding time and improved release predictability across contributors.
projects.next: Introduce observability SLOs and staged environment promotion strategy.

projects.name: Home Finance Tracker
projects.problem: Managing shared household expenses across multiple people requires a centralized, mobile-friendly tool with reliable monthly reconciliation.
projects.solution: A comprehensive ecosystem consisting of a Laravel (PHP) backend API, a modern Vite-based web backoffice, and an Ionic framework mobile app.
projects.result: Enabled consistent expense tracking and monthly reporting with lower manual reconciliation effort.
projects.context: Long-lived private product with iterative improvements and production maintenance.
projects.role: I engineered the full stack, set up the Dockerized local and production environments, and developed the synchronized mobile and web clients.
projects.architecture: Backend API with admin backoffice and mobile client, backed by containerized local/prod environments.
decisions: Separate mobile and backoffice concerns with shared API
Containerize build/run flows for stable environments
Prioritize practical reporting outputs over dashboard complexity
projects.impact: Improved financial visibility for end users and reduced recurring support friction.
projects.next: Add automated anomaly detection and forecast features for monthly cashflow planning.
